headline: Trump announces ceasefire with Iran and offers two-week window for diplomacy
description: The US president, who had vowed to annihilate Iran, announced a ceasefire with Tehran on Tuesday evening, which should lead to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Israel said it supported the agreement but insisted that its operations in Lebanon were unaffected.

headline: French hostages return home after more than three years in Iranian prison
description: Cécile Kohler and Jacques Paris landed in Paris on Wednesday morning, following years of detention on espionage charges.

headline: In Hungary, Vance backs Orban in last-ditch attempt five days before elections
description: The American vice president praised the Hungarian prime minister during a rally in Budapest on Tuesday. In power since 2010, the nationalist leader is not the favorite in the April 12 legislative elections.

headline: In catacombs, museums, and labs, the delicate heritage of human remains
description: The Paris Catacombs reopen to the public on Wednesday, April 8, after five months of renovations. The timing offers a chance to reflect on how human remains are handled in cultural and research institutions. These singular relics pose ethical, technical and legal questions.

headline: 'The US administration's dysfunctions and the Iranian regime's resilience are driving Trump further into an obsession with force'
description: Five weeks into the US bombing campaign against Iran, Donald Trump's trademark unpredictability has been overtaken by the unpredictable dynamics of the war itself, observes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

Are Trump's ideologues intellectuals or imposters?
description: Curtis Yarvin has become a leading figure in the Neo-Reactionary movement (also known as the Dark Enlightenment), which has now made it to the White House. But can this blogger really be considered an intellectual? Political theorist Arnaud Miranda and sociologist Olivier Alexandre debate the origins and roles of one of the thinkers who has Trump's ear.

headline: Sex is an art form hidden in the shadows
description: Moral panic over the pleasures of the flesh prevents us from recognizing the artistic dimension of sexuality, as well as from passing down the best practices, argues Le Monde columnist  Maïa Mazaurette.

headline: Viktor Medvedchuk, the Ukrainian traitor Putin wanted to install in place of Zelensky
description: Captured in April 2022 as he tried to flee Ukraine, the former MP and oligarch now lives in exile in Russia. As a lawyer in Kyiv in 1980, he sent his client, the great Ukrainian poet Vasyl Stus, to his death.

headline: Will Self: 'Our historical condition is a peculiar mixture of omniscience and impotence'
'Can Le Pen and Bardella accept betraying a portion of their own voters to get elected?'
The EU must respond to the challenges posed by Viktor Orban
